page: Microsoft Azure DC32s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DCSv3 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 48 MB L3 cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, with support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F and AVX512_VNNI) and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-threaded and cryptographic capabilities. The server achieved a PassMark CPU score of 49,792.6 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 17,321.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. Cryptographic performance is highlighted by a PassMark CPU encryption test result of 36,894.88. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reached 276.82 GB/sec, and static web serving throughput registered at 47.09 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core metrics, such as the stress-ng single-core CPU performance of 1,897.99 and Geekbench single-core score of 1,686.0, fall within the middle 50% average tier. Large-scale LLM text generation represents a performance bottleneck, with the 70B model text generation speed dropping to a weak 2.73 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DC32s_v3 offers a high-density memory allocation relative to its dedicated compute cores, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of GPU hardware. The lack of local storage requires reliance on external premium storage options, which represents a structural tradeoff. Given the strong performance in PassMark database operations (25,003.69) and high memory capacity, this server is configured for relational databases, memory-cached web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and enterprise application hosting.
description: Microsoft Azure DC32s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DCSv3 family, featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on an x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it provides 256.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The hardware supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core performance, achieving a PassMark CPU score of 49,792.6 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 276.82 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation remain average to weak. This server is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC32s_v3 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 256 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance, suited for databases and high-throughput web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC32s_v3 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 Intel Xeon cores and 256 GB RAM, designed for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-density RAM for demanding database and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
Strong multi-core performance in PassMark and Geekbench testing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration
